== Notes for [[Item:Q1459|Q1459]] ==

<p style="text-align: left;" dir="ltr"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teleost">https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teleost</a></p>
<p><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3" style="font-size: 1.2em; font-family: sans-serif; white-space: normal; word-spacing: 0px; text-transform: none; font-weight: bold; padding-bottom: 0px; font-style: normal; padding-top: 0.5em; orphans: 2; widows: 2; margin: 0.25em 0px; letter-spacing: normal; line-height: 1.6; background-color: #ffffff; text-indent: 0px; font-variant-ligatures: normal; font-variant-caps: normal; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;">
<p><h3 id="Internal_relationships" style="font-family: inherit; font-weight: bold; margin: 0px 0px 0.25em; display: inline; line-height: 1.6; font-stretch: inherit; font-optical-sizing: inherit; font-size-adjust: inherit; font-kerning: inherit; font-feature-settings: inherit; font-variation-settings: inherit; border: 0px; padding: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large;">Internal relationships</span></h3>
<p><span class="mw-editsection" style="font-size: small; font-family: sans-serif; vertical-align: baseline; font-weight: normal; margin-left: 1em; line-height: 0; margin-right: 0px; user-select: none; margin-inline: 1em 0px;"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket" style="margin-right: 0.25em; margin-inline: 0px 0.25em;"><span style="font-size: medium;">[</span></span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; white-space: nowrap; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="Edit section: Internal relationships" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Teleost&amp;action=edit&amp;section=4"><span><span style="font-size: medium;">edit</span></span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket" style="margin-left: 0.25em; margin-inline: 0.25em 0px;"><span style="font-size: medium;">]</span></span></span></div>
<p><p style="font-size: 16px; font-family: sans-serif; white-space: normal; word-spacing: 0px; text-transform: none; font-weight: 400; color: #202122; font-style: normal; orphans: 2; widows: 2; margin: 0.5em 0px 1em; letter-spacing: normal; background-color: #ffffff; text-indent: 0px; font-variant-ligatures: normal; font-variant-caps: normal; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;">The phylogeny of the teleosts has been subject to long debate, without consensus on either their<span>&nbsp;</span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="Phylogenetic tree" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phylogenetic_tree">phylogeny</a><span>&nbsp;</span>or the timing of the emergence of the major groups before the application of modern<span>&nbsp;</span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="DNA" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DNA">DNA</a>-based cladistic analysis. Near et al. (2012) explored the phylogeny and divergence times of every major lineage, analysing the DNA sequences of 9 unlinked genes in 232 species. They obtained well-resolved phylogenies with strong support for the nodes (so, the pattern of branching shown is likely to be correct). They calibrated (set actual values for) branching times in this tree from 36 reliable measurements of absolute time from the fossil record.<sup id="cite_ref-PNAS_16-3" class="reference" style="font-size: 12px; white-space: nowrap; font-weight: normal; font-style: normal; line-height: 1;"><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teleost#cite_note-PNAS-16"><span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">[</span>15<span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">]</span></a></sup><span>&nbsp;</span>The teleosts are divided into the major clades shown on the cladogram,<sup id="cite_ref-20" class="reference" style="font-size: 12px; white-space: nowrap; font-weight: normal; font-style: normal; line-height: 1;"><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teleost#cite_note-20"><span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">[</span>19<span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">]</span></a></sup><span>&nbsp;</span>with dates, following Near et al.<sup id="cite_ref-PNAS_16-4" class="reference" style="font-size: 12px; white-space: nowrap; font-weight: normal; font-style: normal; line-height: 1;"><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teleost#cite_note-PNAS-16"><span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">[</span>15<span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">]</span></a></sup><span>&nbsp;</span>More recent research divide the teleosts into two major groups: Eloposteoglossocephala (Elopomorpha + Osteoglossomorpha) and Clupeocephala (the rest of the teleosts).<sup id="cite_ref-21" class="reference" style="font-size: 12px; white-space: nowrap; font-weight: normal; font-style: normal; line-height: 1;"><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teleost#cite_note-21"><span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">[</span>20<span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-22" class="reference" style="font-size: 12px; white-space: nowrap; font-weight: normal; font-style: normal; line-height: 1;"><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teleost#cite_note-22"><span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">[</span>21<span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">]</span></a></sup></p>
<p><div class="clade" style="font-size: 16px; font-family: sans-serif; white-space: normal; word-spacing: 0px; text-transform: none; font-weight: 400; color: #202122; font-style: normal; orphans: 2; widows: 2; letter-spacing: normal; background-color: #ffffff; text-indent: 0px; font-variant-ligatures: normal; font-variant-caps: normal; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;">&nbsp;</div>
<p><p style="text-align: left;" dir="ltr">&nbsp;</p></p>

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
